name: Fudgy Cocoa Brownies (Low-Cal)
description: A deliberately protein-powder-FREE fudgy brownie - the anti-rubber move after protein-brownie bases kept landing bouncy/dry. Fudge comes from the classic levers: real butter + real milk chocolate chips (fat IS fudgy), cocoa-dominant and flour-light (25g cocoa vs ~31g flour = dense, not cakey), Greek yogurt + almond milk for moisture, espresso to deepen the chocolate. Sweetened with brown sugar (the CHEW lever - molasses = bend + moisture + caramel-against-chocolate) topped off with granulated Splenda. Adapted from an online satiety-optimized recipe (monk fruit swapped for the brown sugar + Splenda combo). Fat runs ~35-40% of calories by design - it is the whole batch's ~25g of fat doing the fudgy work; this is a lean dessert, not a protein bake (~16g protein/batch from egg + yogurt). High carb, low cal, satiating.

steps.instructionText: Whisk the dry ingredients: AP flour, cocoa powder, brown sugar, granulated Splenda, espresso powder, baking soda, and salt. Break up any brown-sugar lumps.
In a second bowl whisk the melted (slightly cooled) butter, egg, Greek yogurt, and almond milk until smooth.

Fold wet into dry until JUST combined - with this little flour there's no gluten to toughen, but don't beat air in; a dense batter bakes a dense brownie. Fold in the chocolate chips. Spread into a small lined pan (mini loaf pan or ~6-inch).

Bake at 350F for ~18-20 min. Pull when the edges are set but a toothpick in the center still comes out with MOIST CRUMBS - a clean toothpick means overbaked. Cool fully in the pan (they set as they cool); cut 4 squares.

notes.text: NO protein powder, on purpose. Whey denaturing in the batter is exactly what made every protein-brownie base bouncy and dry. This base gets dense/fudgy from real fat + low flour instead; don't add a scoop to 'fix' the macros - that recreates the rubber problem. Protein here is ~16g/batch from the egg + yogurt.

Brown sugar is the chew lever - fudgy comes from fat, dense from low flour, but CHEWY comes from dissolved sugar, and molasses adds moisture plus a caramel note against the chocolate. The Splenda tops off sweetness without more calories. If the chew still falls short, shift the ratio toward brown sugar (each extra tbsp ~ +45 cal/batch) before touching anything else.

Overbaking is the killer - with only ~31g flour there is no structure to save a dry bake. Pull at moist crumbs on the toothpick, never clean, and let them set as they cool. Fudgy brownies always look slightly underdone hot.
Fat runs ~35-40% of calories - deliberate. The batch's ~25g of fat (butter + chips + egg) IS the fudge; this is filed as a lean dessert (high carb, low cal, satiating), not a protein bake.
